title: AI Data Privacy
description: What are the current legal requirements for using personal data to train machine learning models in the EU and US?
narrative: The user is looking for up-to-date information on privacy laws and regulations that apply to the collection, processing, and use of personal data for AI training. Relevant content should cover the GDPR in Europe, the CCA​P/CPRA in California, and any emerging federal proposals in the United States, detailing obligations such as consent, data minimization, purpose limitation, data subject rights, and compliance best practices. Exclude general AI tutorials, unrelated privacy news, or non‑legal discussions.

title: Solar Incentives
description: What federal and state incentives are currently available for residential solar panel installation in the United States?
narrative: The user wants comprehensive, up-to-date information on financial support for homeowners installing solar photovoltaic systems, including tax credits, rebates, grants, low‑interest loans, and other programs. Relevant content must detail the specific incentives, eligibility requirements, application procedures, and any recent changes or expiration dates as of 2024, covering both nationwide and state‑specific offerings.

title: Renewable Energy Policy
description: What are the key factors influencing the adoption of renewable energy policies in European Union member states since 2010?
narrative: Relevant documents should describe legislative frameworks, economic incentives, stakeholder roles, and case studies of renewable energy implementation within EU countries after 2010. Sources that focus on non‑EU regions, pre‑2010 policies, or unrelated energy sectors (e.g., fossil fuel extraction) are not relevant.

title: Urban Heat Islands
description: What mitigation strategies are most effective for reducing urban heat islands in mid-sized cities?
narrative: The user seeks detailed information on practical, evidence‑based approaches to lessen the urban heat island effect specifically in cities with populations between 100,000 and 500,000. Relevant documents should describe mitigation techniques (e.g., green roofs, reflective pavements, increased vegetation, district cooling), provide quantitative results or case‑study evaluations, and discuss implementation challenges and cost considerations. Preference is given to peer‑reviewed studies, government reports, or reputable industry analyses published within the last ten years. Broad climate‑change overviews or strategies targeting only megacities or rural areas are not relevant.

title: Renewable Energy Policy
description: What are the latest policy measures in the United States and European Union that affect renewable energy investment and how do they compare?
narrative: The user seeks documents describing recent (2023‑2024) legislation, subsidies, tax incentives, and regulatory frameworks in the US and EU that promote renewable energy. Relevant content must detail the policy provisions, contrast the two regions, and include analysis of the likely impact on investors and project developers.

title: Solar Incentives
description: What financial incentives are available for residential solar panel installations in the United States in 2024?
narrative: The user is looking for current information on federal, state, and local subsidies, tax credits, rebates, and other financial programs that homeowners can use to offset the cost of installing solar panels in 2024. Relevant documents should detail eligibility criteria, application procedures, program amounts, and any deadlines or restrictions.

title: Urban Heat Islands
description: How do urban heat islands affect energy consumption in high-density residential neighborhoods during summer?
narrative: The user is looking for scholarly articles, government reports, or credible data sets that examine the impact of urban heat island phenomena on residential electricity and heating/cooling usage in densely populated city districts during the summer season. Relevant material should provide quantitative analysis, modeling results, or case studies that link temperature differentials caused by urban heat islands to changes in energy demand, and may also discuss mitigation measures such as green roofs, reflective surfaces, or urban planning interventions.

title: Solar Incentives
description: What federal and state incentives are available for installing residential solar panels in the United States?
narrative: The user wants current, detailed information on financial incentives (tax credits, rebates, grants, loan programs) that apply to homeowners who install solar photovoltaic systems. Relevant documents must list specific programs, eligibility requirements, incentive amounts, and expiration dates for both federal initiatives (e.g., the Investment Tax Credit) and state or utility‑level programs. Sources that only describe solar technology, non‑residential incentives, or general environmental benefits are not relevant.

title: Dysarthria Rehabilitation Outcomes
description: What are the most effective speech‑therapy approaches for improving functional communication in adults with dysarthria?
narrative: The user is looking for evidence‑based information on speech‑therapy interventions that have shown measurable improvements in intelligibility, speaking rate, and quality of life for adult dysarthria patients. Relevant material should describe specific therapeutic techniques, the populations studied, quantitative or qualitative outcomes, and any factors that influence treatment success.

title: Carbon Tax Impact
description: How have carbon taxes affected electricity prices for residential consumers in Germany since 2020?
narrative: The user is looking for detailed analyses, reports, or empirical studies that examine changes in household electricity costs in Germany after the implementation of carbon pricing mechanisms in 2020 and later. Relevant documents should provide quantitative price data, discuss the role of carbon taxes in the observed trends, and, if possible, compare pre‑ and post‑tax periods or examine regional variations within Germany. Sources that merely mention carbon taxes without linking them to residential electricity pricing, or that focus on other countries, are not relevant.

title: Urban Heat Islands
description: What are the primary causes and mitigation strategies for urban heat islands in mid-sized cities?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive information on the factors that contribute to the formation of urban heat islands, particularly focusing on mid-sized cities (population 100,000‑500,000). Relevant documents should discuss temperature differentials, land use patterns, building materials, and vegetation loss, as well as effective mitigation measures such as green roofs, reflective surfaces, and urban planning policies. Sources that only address rural heat effects, generic climate change topics, or case studies of megacities (>1 million) are not considered relevant.

title: Urban Heat Islands
description: How do urban heat island effects vary across different city sizes and climates, and what mitigation strategies are most effective?
narrative: The user wants comprehensive information on urban heat island (UHI) phenomena, including quantitative analyses that compare UHI intensity in small, medium, and large cities across multiple climate zones. Relevant sources should discuss the underlying physical and socioeconomic causes, evaluate mitigation techniques such as green roofs, increased vegetation, reflective or cool pavements, and policy measures, and provide evidence on their effectiveness, cost, scalability, and implementation challenges.

title: Solar Incentive Policies
description: What recent policies and incentives support residential solar installations in the United States?
narrative: The user seeks up-to-date information (2022‑2024) on federal, state, and local policies that encourage homeowners to install solar panels, including tax credits, rebates, net‑metering rules, financing programs, and any effectiveness assessments. Relevant documents should describe the policy mechanisms, eligibility criteria, geographic scope, and documented impact on solar adoption rates, and should come from governmental, industry, or reputable research sources.

title: Urban Heat Islands
description: What are effective mitigation strategies for urban heat islands in mid-sized cities?
narrative: The user is looking for authoritative sources—such as peer‑reviewed articles, government reports, or case‑study publications—that describe concrete measures to reduce the urban heat island effect in cities with populations between 100,000 and 500,000. Relevant content should include evaluations of green infrastructure (e.g., trees, green roofs), reflective or cool roofing materials, urban design interventions, and documented impacts on temperature reduction or energy savings. Materials that only discuss theoretical climate models without actionable recommendations, or that focus on mega‑cities exceeding 5 million residents, are not relevant.

title: Renewable Incentives

narrative: The user wants a thorough, current overview of financial incentives for homeowners installing solar PV systems, including federal tax credits, state rebates, grants, and any recent policy updates. description: What federal and state policies were enacted in the United States from 2010 to 2020 to incentivize residential solar power adoption?

Sources that merely mention solar energy without detailed policy information, or that cover periods outside the specified range, are not relevant.

title: Renewable Energy Policy

title: Carbon Tax Impact
description: What are the economic and environmental effects of implementing a carbon tax in the United States?
narrative: The user is seeking comprehensive analyses, case studies, and empirical data that evaluate both the economic outcomes (e.g., changes in GDP, employment, energy prices) and environmental outcomes (e.g., CO2 emissions reductions, air quality improvements) resulting from a federal carbon tax. Relevant documents should discuss policy design, implementation timeline, sector-specific impacts, and comparisons with alternative climate policies, while excluding purely theoretical discussions without real-world evidence.

title: Renewable Energy Incentives
description: What federal tax credits are available for residential solar panel installations in the United States?
narrative: The user is looking for current information on U.S. federal tax incentives that apply to homeowners who install solar panels, including the amount of the credit, eligibility requirements, and how to claim it. title: Renewable Energy Policy
description: What recent policy mechanisms have European countries adopted to incentivize residential solar panel installations?
narrative: The user seeks documents describing specific legislative or regulatory measures introduced in Europe since 2018 that aim to increase rooftop solar adoption, such as subsidies, tax credits, net‑metering rules, and related case studies, with details on the policy provisions, the countries involved, and evidence of their effectiveness.

title: Urban Food Systems
description: How can cities integrate vertical farms to improve food security and reduce carbon emissions?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive information on the adoption of vertical farming within urban environments. Relevant material should include technical designs, scalability analyses, case studies or pilot projects, quantitative assessments of food‑security impacts, carbon‑footprint reductions, economic viability, and policy or regulatory frameworks that support implementation. Documents that only mention vertical farms in passing or lack detailed evaluation of these criteria are not relevant.

title: Urban Heat Islands
description: How do urban heat island effects differ across cities of varying sizes and climate zones?
narrative: The user wants detailed information on the causes, measurement techniques, and mitigation strategies for urban heat islands, focusing on comparative analyses between small, medium, and large cities in different climatic regions. Relevant material should include scientific studies, government reports, or policy documents that discuss temperature differentials, contributing factors (e.g., land‑use, building materials), and evaluated interventions such as green roofs, tree canopy expansion, or high‑albedo surfaces.

title: Renewable Energy Policy
description: What are the key challenges and policy mechanisms for integrating large-scale renewable energy into existing power grids in developing countries?
narrative: The user is seeking comprehensive information on how developing nations can incorporate significant amounts of renewable electricity (solar, wind, hydro, etc.) into their current grid infrastructures. Relevant documents should discuss technical integration issues (e.g., intermittency, storage, grid stability), economic and financing considerations, regulatory and policy frameworks, and illustrative case studies or best‑practice examples from developing countries. Materials that focus solely on renewable technology without addressing grid integration, or that pertain only to developed nations, are not relevant.

title: Renewable Energy Policy
description: What are the major challenges facing the implementation of renewable energy policies in developing countries?
narrative: The user is looking for scholarly articles, policy reports, or case studies that discuss economic, regulatory, and infrastructural obstacles to adopting renewable energy in developing nations. Relevant documents should address at least one specific developing country, provide evidence‑based analysis of the barriers, and suggest possible solutions or policy recommendations.

narrative: The user is looking for documents that describe federal or state policies enacted after 2020 aimed at increasing residential solar installations. title: Urban Heat Islands
description: What mitigation strategies are most effective for reducing urban heat island effects in high‑density cities?
narrative: The user seeks recent research, case studies, or policy reports that describe, evaluate, and compare mitigation techniques for urban heat islands—such as green roofs, increased tree canopy, reflective or permeable paving, and district cooling systems. Relevant documents should present quantitative results (e.g., temperature reduction, energy savings), discuss implementation challenges, and consider different climatic or geographic contexts. Articles that only measure temperature trends without addressing mitigation, or that focus on unrelated urban issues, are not relevant.

title: Grid Storage Solutions
description: What are the most promising large‑scale energy storage technologies for integrating renewable power into electricity grids?
narrative: The user is looking for up‑to‑date, comprehensive information on technologies that can store electricity at grid scale to support intermittent renewable sources. Relevant documents should describe the principle of operation, scalability, cost per kWh, round‑trip efficiency, deployment status, and environmental impacts of options such as pumped hydro, lithium‑ion batteries, flow batteries, compressed air, hydrogen, and emerging thermal or super‑capacitor systems. Sources that provide comparative analyses, case studies, or recent pilot projects are especially pertinent, while generic overviews of small‑scale storage or unrelated energy topics are not relevant.

title: Solar Incentive Policies
description: What policy mechanisms have proven most effective at increasing residential solar adoption in the United States?
narrative: The user is looking for recent studies, government reports, and case analyses that evaluate the impact of various incentives—such as tax credits, rebates, net‑metering rules, and financing programs—on residential solar installations. Relevant material should include quantitative outcomes (e.g., installed capacity growth, cost‑benefit ratios), discussions of scalability and equity, and concrete policy recommendations or lessons learned.

title: Renewable Energy Policy
description: What are the most effective policy mechanisms for increasing renewable energy adoption in developing countries?
narrative: The user seeks scholarly articles, reports, or case studies that describe specific policy tools—such as feed‑in tariffs, subsidies, tax incentives, and regulatory frameworks—that have successfully stimulated renewable energy deployment in developing nations. title: Urban Heat Islands
description: What are the effects of vegetation cover and building materials on urban heat islands in various climate zones?

Relevant documents should provide quantitative measurements, modeling results, or case‑study analyses, and preferably compare outcomes across multiple climate zones. Sources that discuss mitigation strategies, policy recommendations, or health impacts linked to these factors are also considered relevant, while generic discussions of heat without specific reference to urban environments or mitigation tactics are not.

title: Urban Heat Islands
description: What mitigation strategies are most effective for reducing urban heat island effects in densely populated cities?

title: Remote Work Productivity
description: How does remote work affect employee productivity across different industries?
narrative: The user is looking for evidence‑based analyses, studies, or reports that examine the impact of remote work on employee productivity, with a focus on comparisons between sectors, the metrics used to measure productivity, and contextual factors such as management practices, technology adoption, and work‑life balance. title: Renewable Energy Policy
description: What are the key challenges and best practices for implementing renewable energy policies at the municipal level?

title: EU Renewable Policy
